lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: This is private property! Please leave!

sections.title: Talking to Frank, David, or Bob
Talking to Anna, Carol, Mary, or Elizabeth

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: I'm far too upset to talk to random people right now.

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: I have no interest in talking to gawkers.

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: The guards told me not to talk to anyone.

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: Have you no shame? We are all grieving at the moment.

sections.title: Investigating the gate, sacks, drain, or barrels

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on here?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh, it's terrible! Lord Sinclair has been murdered and we don't have any clues as to who or why. We're totally baffled!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: If you can help us we will be very grateful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, I'll help!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Thanks a lot!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing to help?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Look around and investigate who might be responsible. The Sarge said every murder leaves clues to who done it, but frankly we're out of our depth here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You should do your own dirty work.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Get lost then, this is private property! ...Unless you'd like to be taken in for questioning yourself?

sections.title: Talking to the Guard after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing to help?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Look around and investigate who might be responsible. The Sarge said every murder leaves clues to who done it, but frankly we're out of our depth here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did Lord Sinclair die?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Well, it's all very mysterious. Mary, the maid, found the body in the study next to his bedroom on the east wing of the ground floor.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: The door was found locked from the inside, and he seemed to have been stabbed, but there was an odd smell in the room. Frankly, I'm stumped.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know who did it!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Really? That was quick work! Who?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was an intruder!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: That's what we were thinking too. That someone broke in to steal something, was discovered by Lord Sinclair, stabbed him and ran.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: It's odd that apparently nothing was stolen though... Find out something has been stolen,

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: and the case is closed, but the murdered man was a friend of the King, and it's more than my job's worth not to investigate fully.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The butler did it!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I hope you have proof to that effect. We have to arrest someone for this and it seems to me that only the actual murderer would gain by falsely accusing someone.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Although having said that the butler is kind of shifty looking...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was one of the servants!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh really? Which one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was one of the women

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Great work. Show me the evidence, and we'll take them to the dungeons.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You DO have evidence of their crime, right?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Tch. You wouldn't last a day in the guards with sloppy thinking like that. Come see me when you have some proof of your accusations.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Great work. Show me the evidence, and we'll take them to the dungeons.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was one of the men...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Great work. Show me the evidence, and we'll take them to the dungeons.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Great work. Show me the evidence, and we'll take them to the dungeons.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Great work. Show me the evidence, and we'll take them to the dungeons.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Great work. Show me the evidence, and we'll take them to the dungeons.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was one of his family!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh really? Which one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was one of the women...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Great work. Show me the evidence, and we'll take them to the dungeons.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Great work. Show me the evidence, and we'll take them to the dungeons.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Great work. Show me the evidence, and we'll take them to the dungeons.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was one of the men...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Great work. Show me the evidence, and we'll take them to the dungeons.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Great work. Show me the evidence, and we'll take them to the dungeons.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Great work. Show me the evidence, and we'll take them to the dungeons.

sections.title: Picking up thread from the ground

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Oh really? What do you want to know then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you think was responsible?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: It was clearly an intruder.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I don't think it was.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: It was one of our lazy servants then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where were you when the murder happened?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: In the library. No one else was there so you'll just have to take my word for it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you recognise this thread?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Not really, no. Thread is fairly common.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I suppose I had better talk to you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you think was responsible?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I don't really care as long as no one thinks it's me. Maybe it was that strange poison seller who headed towards the seers village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where were you when the murder happened?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I was walking by myself in the garden.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And can anyone vouch for that?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: No. But I was.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you recognise this thread?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: It's some thread. great clue. No, really.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Well, ask what you want to know then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you think was responsible?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: I don't know. I think it's very convenient that you have arrived here so soon after it happened. Maybe it was you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where were you when the murder happened?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Why? Are you accusing me of something? You seem to have a very high opinion of yourself I was in my room if you must know, alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you recognise this thread?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: It's some thread. Sorry, do you have a point here? Or do you just enjoy wasting peoples time?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: And? Make this quick, I have better things to do than be interrogated by halfwits all day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you think was responsible?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: I don't really know or care. Frankly, the old man deserved to die.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: There was a suspicious red headed man who came to the house the other day selling poison now I think about it. Last I saw he was headed towards the tavern in the Seers village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where were you when the murder happened?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: That is none of your business. Are we finished now, or are you just going to stand there irritating me with your idiotic questions all day?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you recognise this thread?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: No. Can I go yet? Your face irritates me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: What's so important you need to bother me with then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you think was responsible?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Could have been anyone. The old man was an idiot. He's been asking for it for years.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where were you when the murder happened?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: I was out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Care to be any more specific?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Not really. I don't have to justify myself to the likes of you, you know. I know the King personally you know. Now are we finished here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you recognise this thread?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: It's some thread. You're not very good at this whole investigation thing are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Good for you. Now what do you want?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: ...And can you spare me any money? I'm a little short...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you think was responsible?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: You don't know how long it takes an inheritance to come through do you? I could really use that money pretty soon...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where were you when the murder happened?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: I don't know, somewhere around here probably.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Could you spare me a few coins? I'll be able to pay you double tomorrow it's just there's this poker night tonight in town...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you recognise this thread?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: It looks like thread to me, but I'm not exactly an expert. Is it worth something? Can I have it? Actually, can you spare me a few gold?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you think is responsible?

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: Oh... I really couldn't say. I wouldn't really want to point any fingers at anybody. If I had to make a guess I'd have to say it was probably Bob though.

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: I saw him arguing with Lord Sinclair about some missing silverware from the Kitchen. It was a very heated argument.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where were you at the time of the murder?

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: Me? I was sound asleep here in the servants Quarters. It's very hard work as a handyman around here, There's always something to do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you hear any suspicious noises at all?

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: Hmmm..... No, I didn't, but I sleep very soundly at night.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you didn't hear any sounds of a struggle or any barking from the guard dog next to his study window?

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: Now you mention it, no. It is odd I didn't hear anything like that. But I do sleep very soundly as I said and wouldn't necessarily have heard it if there was any such noise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm investigating the murder up at the Sinclair place.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Murder is it? Well, I'm not really surprised...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the Sinclairs?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Well, what do you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Lord Sinclair.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Old Lord Sinclair was a great man with a lot of respect in these parts. More than his worthless children have anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: His children? They have something to gain by his death?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Yes. You could say that. Not that I'm one to gossip.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do the Sinclairs live so far from town?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Well, they used to live in the big castle, but old Lord Sinclair gave it up so that those strange knights could live there instead. So the king built him a new house to the North.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: It's more cramped than his old place, but he seemed to like it. His children were furious at him for doing it though!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about his sons?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: His sons eh? They all have their own skeletons in their cupboards. You'll have to be more specific. Who are you interested in exactly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Bob.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Bob is an odd character indeed... I'm not one to gossip, but I heard Bob is addicted to Tea. He can't make

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: it through the day without having at least 20 cups!

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: You might not think that's such a big thing, but he has spent thousands of gold to feed his habit!

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: At one point he stole a lot of silverware from the kitchen and pawned it just so he could afford to buy his daily tea allowance.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: If his father ever found out, he would be in so much trouble... he might even get disowned!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about David.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: David... oh David... not many people know this, but David really has an anger problem. He's always screaming and shouting

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: at the household servants when he's angry, and they live in a state of fear, always walking on eggshells around him, but none of them have the courage

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: to talk to his father about his behaviour. If they did, Lord Sinclair would almost certainly

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: kick him out of the house, as some of the servants have been there longer than he has, and he definitely has no right to treat them like he does... but I'm not one to gossip about people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Frank.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: I'm not one to talk ill of people behind their back, but Frank is a real piece of work. He is an absolutely terrible gambler... he can't pass 2 dogs in the street without putting a bet on which one will bark first!

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: He has already squandered all of his allowance, and I heard he had stolen a number of paintings of his fathers to sell to try and cover his debts, but he still owes a lot of

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: people a lot of money. If his father ever found out, he would stop his income, and then he would be in serious trouble!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about his daughters?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: His daughters eh? They're all nasty pieces of work. which of them specifically did you want to know about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Anna.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Anna... ah yes... Anna has 2 great loves:

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Sewing and gardening. But one thing she has kept secret is that she once had an affair with Stanford the gardener, and tried to get him fired when they broke up,

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: by killing all of the flowers in the garden. If her father ever found out she had done that he would be so furious he would probably disown her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Carol.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Oh Carol... she is such a fool. You didn't hear this from me, but I heard a while ago she was conned out of a lot of money by a travelling salesman who sold her a box full

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: of beans by telling her they were magic. But they weren't. She sold some rare books from the library to cover her debts, but

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: her father would be incredibly annoyed if he ever found out - he might even throw her out of the house!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Elizabeth.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Elizabeth? Elizabeth has a strange problem... She cannot help herself, but is always stealing small objects - it's pretty sad that she is rich enough to afford to buy things, but would rather steal them instead.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Now, I don't want to spread stories, but I heard she even stole a silver needle from her father that had great sentimental value for him.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: He was devastated when it was lost, and cried for a week thinking he had lost it!

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: If he ever found out that it was her who had stolen it he would go absolutely mental, maybe even disowning her!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you think was responsible?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Well, I guess it could have been an intruder, but with that big guard dog of theirs I seriously doubt it. I suspect it was someone closer to home...

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Especially as I heard that the poison salesman in the Seers' village made a big sale to one of the family the other day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think the butler did it.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: And I think you've been reading too many cheap detective novels. Hobbes is kind of uptight, but his loyalty to old Lord Sinclair is beyond question.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am so confused about who did it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Think you could give me any hints?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Well, I don't know if it's related, but I heard from that Poison Salesman in town that he sold some poison to one of the Sinclair family

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: the other day. I don't think he has any stock left now though...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you think is responsible?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: Well, in my considered opinion it must be David. The man is nothing more than a bully And I happen to know that poor Lord Sinclair and David had a massive argument in the living

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: room about the way he treats the staff, the other day. I did not intend to overhear their conversation, but they were shouting so loudly I could not help but Overhear it. David definitely used the words

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: 'I am going to kill you!' as well. I think he should be the prime suspect. He has a nasty temper that one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where were you at the time of the murder?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: I was assisting the cook with the evening meal. I gave Mary His Lordships' dinner, and sent her to take it to him, then heard the scream as she found the body.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you hear any suspicious noises at all?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: How do you mean 'suspicious'?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any sounds of a struggle with Lord Sinclair?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: No, I definitely didn't hear anything like that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about the guard dog barking at all?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: You know, now you come to mention it I don't believe I did. I suppose that is Proof enough that it could not have been an intruder who is responsible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you think is responsible?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: Elizabeth.

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: Her father confronted her about her constant petty thieving, and was devestated to find she had stolen a silver needle which had meant a lot to him.

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: You could hear their argument from Lumbridge!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where were you at the time of the murder?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: I was right here with Hobbes and Mary. You can't suspect me surely!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you hear any suspicious noises at all?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: suspicious? what do you mean suspicious?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any sounds of a struggle with an intruder for example?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: No, I'm sure I don't recall any such thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about the guard dog barking at an intruder?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: No, I didn't. If you don't have anything else to ask can You go and leave me alone now? I have a lot of cooking to do for this evening.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you think is responsible?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Oh I don't know... Frank was acting kind of funny... After that big argument him and the Lord had the other day by the beehive... so

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: I guess maybe him... but it's really scary to think someone here might have been responsible. I actually hope it was a burglar...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where were you at the time of the murder?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: I was with Hobbes and Louisa in the Kitchen helping to prepare Lord Sinclair's meal, and then when I took it to his study... I saw... oh, it was horrible... he was....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you hear any suspicious noises at all?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: I don't really remember hearing anything out of the ordinary.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No sounds of a struggle then?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: No, I don't remember hearing anything like that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about the guard dog barking?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Oh that horrible dog is always barking at nothing but I don't think I did...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you think is responsible?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: Honestly? I think it was Carol.

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: I saw her in a huge argument with Lord Sinclair in the library the other day. It was something to do with stolen books. She definitely seemed upset enough to have done it afterwards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where were you at the time of the murder?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: I was in town at the Inn. When I got back the house was swarming with guards who told me what had happened. Sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you hear any suspicious noises at all?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: Well, like what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any sounds of a struggle with Lord Sinclair?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: No, I don't remember hearing anything like that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about the guard dog barking at all?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: I hear him bark all the time. It's one of his favorite things to do. I can't say I did the night of the murder though as I wasn't close enough to hear either way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you think is responsible?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: It was Anna. She is seriously unbalanced. She trashed the garden once then tried to blame it on me! I bet it was her. It's just the kind of thing she'd do!

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: She really hates me and was arguing with Lord Sinclair about trashing the garden a few days ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where were you at the time of the murder?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: Right here, by my little shed. It's very cosy to sit and think in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you hear any suspicious noises at all?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: Not that I remember.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So no sounds of a struggle between Lord Sinclair and an intruder?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: Not to the best of my recollection.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about the guard dog barking?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: Not that I can recall.

sections.title: Using empty pot on flour barrel
Using silver pot or pungent pot on flour barrel
Using flypaper on floured silver items
Using Anna's print on unknown print
Using Bob's print on unknown print
Using Carol's print on unknown print
Using David's print on unknown print
Using Elizabeth's print on unknown print
Using Frank's print on unknown print

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm investigating the murder at the Sinclair house.

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: There was a murder at the Sinclair House??? That's terrible! And I was only there the other day too! They bought the last of my Patented Multi Purpose Poison!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Patented Multi Purpose Poison?

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Aaaaah... a miracle of modern apothecaries!

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: This exclusive concoction has been tested on all known forms of life and been proven to kill them all in varying dilutions from cockroaches to king dragons!

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: So incredibly versatile, it can be used as pest control, a cleansing agent, drain cleaner, metal polish and washes whiter than white,

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: all with our uniquely fragrant concoction that is immediately recognisable across the land as Peter Potter's Patented Poison potion!!!

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: I'd love to sell you some but I've sold out recently. That's just how good it is! Three hundred and twenty eight people in this area alone cannot be wrong!

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Nine out of Ten poisoners prefer it in controlled tests!

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Can I help you with anything else?

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Perhaps I can take your name and add it to our mailing list of poison users? We will only send you information related to the use of poison and other Peter Potter Products!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... no, it's ok. Really.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who did you sell Poison to at the house?

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Well, Peter Potter's Patented Multi Purpose Poison is a product of such obvious quality that I am glad to say I managed to sell a bottle to each of the Sinclairs!

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Anna, Bob, Carol, David, Elizabeth and Frank all bought a bottle! In fact they bought the last of my supplies!

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Maybe I can take your name and address and I will personally come and visit you when stocks return?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... no, it's ok.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I buy some Poison?

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: I'm afraid I am totally out of stock at the moment after my successful trip to the Sinclairs' House the other day.

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: But don't worry! Our factories are working overtime to produce Peter Potter's Patented Multi Purpose Poison!

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Possibly the finest multi purpose poison and cleaner yet available to the general market.

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: And its unique fragrance makes it the number one choice for cleaners and exterminators the whole country over!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have this pot I found at the murder scene...

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Hmmm... yes, that smells exactly like my Patented Multi Purpose Poison, but I don't see how it could be. It quite clearly says on the label of all bottles

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: 'Not to be taken internally - EXTREMELY POISONOUS'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps someone else put it in his wine?

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Yes... I suppose that could have happened...

sections.title: Talking to the Poison Salesman if the player also has progress in Fremennik Trials

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm investigating the murder at the Sinclair house.

sections.title: Talking to Anna after learning about the poison

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Oh really? What do you want to know then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why'd you buy poison the other day?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: That useless Gardener Stanford has let his compost heap fester. It's an eyesore to the garden! So I bought some poison from a travelling salesman so that I could kill off some of the wildlife living in it.

sections.title: Talking to Bob after learning about the poison

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I suppose I had better talk to you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why'd you buy poison the other day?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: What's it to you anyway?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: If you absolutely must know, we had a problem with the beehive in the garden, and as all of our servants are so pathetically useless, I decided I would deal with it myself. So I did.

sections.title: Talking to Carol after learning about the poison

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Well, ask what you want to know then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why'd you buy poison the other day?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: I don't see what on earth it has to do with you, but the drain outside was

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: blocked, and as nobody else here has the intelligence to even unblock a simple drain I felt I had to do it myself.

sections.title: Talking to David after learning about the poison

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: And? Make this quick, I have better things to do than be interrogated by halfwits all day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why'd you buy poison the other day?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: There was a nest of spiders upstairs between the two servants' quarters. Obviously I had to kill them before our pathetic servants whined at my father some more.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Honestly, it's like they expect to be treated like royalty! If I had my way I would fire the whole workshy lot of them!

sections.title: Talking to Elizabeth after learning about the poison

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: What's so important you need to bother me with then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why'd you buy poison the other day?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: There was a nest of mosquitos under the fountain in the garden, which I killed with poison the other day. You can see for yourself if you're capable of managing that, which I somehow doubt.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hate mosquitos!

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Doesn't everyone?

sections.title: Talking to Frank after learning about the poison

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Good for you. Now what do you want?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: ...And can you spare me any money? I'm a little short...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why'd you buy poison the other day?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Would you like to buy some? I'm kind of strapped for cash right now, I'll sell it to you cheap, it's hardly been used at all.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: I just used a bit to clean that family crest outside up a bit. Do you think I can get much money For the family crest, actually? It's cleaned up a bit now.

sections.title: Talking to Donovan after learning about the poison

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know why so much poison was bought recently?

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: Well, I do know Frank bought some poison recently to clean the family crest that's outside.

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: It's very old and rusty, and I couldn't clean it myself, so he said he would buy some cleaner and clean it himself. He probably just got some from that Poison Salesman who came to the door the other day...

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: You'd really have to ask him though.

sections.title: Talking to Hobbes after learning about the poison

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know why so much poison was bought recently?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: Well, I do know that Elizabeth was extremely annoyed by the mosquito nest under the fountain in the garden, and was going to do something about it. I suspect any poison she bought would have been

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: enough to get rid of it. A Good job too, I hate mosquitos.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, so do I.

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: You'd really have to ask her though.

sections.title: Talking to Louisa after learning about the poison

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know why so much poison was bought recently?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: I told Carol to buy some from that strange poison salesman and clean the drains before they began to smell any worse. She was the one who blocked them in the first place with a load

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: of beans that she bought for some reason. There were far too many to eat, and they were almost rotten when she bought them anyway! You'd really have to ask her though.

sections.title: Talking to Mary after learning about the poison

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know why so much poison was bought recently?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: I overheard Anna saying to Stanford that if he didn't do something about the state of his compost heap, she was going to.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: She really doesn't get on well with Stanford. I really have no idea why. You'd really have to ask her though.

sections.title: Talking to Pierre after learning about the poison

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know why so much poison was bought recently?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: Well, I know David said that he was going to do something about the spiders' nest that's between the two servants' quarters upstairs.

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: He made a big deal about it to Mary the Maid, calling her useless and incompetent. I felt quite sorry for her actually. You'd really have to ask him though.

sections.title: Talking to Stanford after learning about the poison

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help the guards with their investigation.

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know why so much poison was bought recently?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: Well, Bob mentioned to me the other day he wanted to get rid of the bees in that hive over there. I think I saw him buying poison

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: from that poison salesman the other day. I assume it was to sort out those bees. You'd really have to ask him though.

sections.title: Investigating compost heap after questioning the Sinclairs
Investigating beehive after questioning the Sinclairs
Investigating kitchen drain after questioning the Sinclairs
Investigating the spiders nest after questioning the Sinclairs
Investigating fountain after questioning the Sinclairs
Investigating family crest after questioning the Sinclairs
Talking to the Guard with some but not all evidence

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know who did it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have proof that it wasn't any of the servants!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All the servants dress in black so it couldn't have been one of them.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: That's some good work there. I guess it wasn't a servant. You still haven't proved who did do it though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have proof that Anna is lying about the poison.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh really? How did you get that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have proof that Bob is lying about the poison.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh really? How did you get that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have proof that Carol is lying about the poison.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh really? How did you get that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have proof that David is lying about the poison.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh really? How did you get that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have proof that Elizabeth is lying about the poison.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh really? How did you get that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have proof that Frank is lying about the poison.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh really? How did you get that?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hmm. That's some good detective work there. We need more evidence before we can close the case though. Keep up the good work!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the finger prints of the culprit!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have Anna's finger prints here. You can see for yourself they match the finger prints on the murder weapon exactly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have Bob's finger prints here. You can see for yourself they match the finger prints on the murder weapon exactly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have Carol's finger prints here. You can see for yourself they match the finger prints on the murder weapon exactly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have David's finger prints here. You can see for yourself they match the finger prints on the murder weapon exactly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have Elizabeth's finger prints here. You can see for yourself they match the finger prints on the murder weapon exactly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have Frank's finger prints here. You can see for yourself they match the finger prints on the murder weapon exactly.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: ... I'm impressed. How on earth did you think of something like that? I've never heard of such a technique for finding criminals before!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: This will come in very handy in the future but we can't arrest someone on just this. I'm afraid you'll still need to find more evidence before we can close this case completely.

sections.title: Talking to the Guard with all evidence

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know who did it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have conclusive proof who the killer was.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You do? That's excellent work. Let's hear it then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think it was an intruder, and I don't think Lord Sinclair was killed by being stabbed.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hmmm? Really? Why not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nobody heard the guard dog barking, which it would have if it had been an intruder who was responsible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nobody heard any signs of a struggle either. I think the knife was there to throw suspicion away from the real culprit.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes, that makes sense. But who did do it then?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes, I'd have to agree with that... but we need more evidence!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Excellent work - have you considered a career as a detective? But I'm afraid it's still not quite enough...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes. There's no doubt about it. It must have been Anna who killed her father. All of the guards must congratulate you on your excellent work in helping us to solve this case.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes, I'd have to agree with that... but we need more evidence!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Excellent work - have you considered a career as a detective? But I'm afraid it's still not quite enough...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes. There's no doubt about it. It must have been Bob who killed his father. All of the guards must congratulate you on your excellent work in helping us to solve this case.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes, I'd have to agree with that... but we need more evidence!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Excellent work - have you considered a career as a detective? But I'm afraid it's still not quite enough...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes. There's no doubt about it. It must have been Carol who killed her father. All of the guards must congratulate you on your excellent work in helping us to solve this case.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes, I'd have to agree with that... but we need more evidence!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Excellent work - have you considered a career as a detective? But I'm afraid it's still not quite enough...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes. There's no doubt about it. It must have been David who killed his father. All of the guards must congratulate you on your excellent work in helping us to solve this case.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes, I'd have to agree with that... but we need more evidence!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Excellent work - have you considered a career as a detective? But I'm afraid it's still not quite enough...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes. There's no doubt about it. It must have been Elizabeth who killed her father. All of the guards must congratulate you on your excellent work in helping us to solve this case.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes, I'd have to agree with that... but we need more evidence!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Excellent work - have you considered a career as a detective? But I'm afraid it's still not quite enough...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes. There's no doubt about it. It must have been Frank who killed his father. All of the guards must congratulate you on your excellent work in helping us to solve this case.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: We don't have many murders here in Gielinor and I'm afraid we wouldn't have been able to solve it by ourselves. We will hold her here under house arrest until such time as we bring her to trial.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You have our gratitude, and I'm sure the rest of the family's as well, in helping to apprehend the murderer. I'll just take the evidence from you now.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Please accept this reward from the family!

sections.title: Talking to the Guard after the quest

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Excellent work on solving the murder! All of the guards I know are very impressed, and don't worry, we have the murderer under guard until they can be taken to trial.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything else I can do?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: No, your work here is done.

sections.title: Talking to Anna after the quest

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Apparently you aren't as stupid as you look.

sections.title: Talking to Bob after the quest

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Apparently you aren't as stupid as you look.

sections.title: Talking to Carol after the quest

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Apparently you aren't as stupid as you look.

sections.title: Talking to David after the quest

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Apparently you aren't as stupid as you look.

sections.title: Talking to Elizabeth after the quest

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Apparently you aren't as stupid as you look.

sections.title: Talking to Frank after the quest

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Apparently you aren't as stupid as you look.

sections.title: Talking to Donovan after the quest

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: Thank you for all your help in solving the murder.

sections.title: Talking to Hobbes after the quest

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: Thank you for all your help in solving the murder.

sections.title: Talking to Louisa after the quest

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: Thank you for all your help in solving the murder.

sections.title: Talking to Mary after the quest

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Thank you for all your help in solving the murder.

sections.title: Talking to Stanford after the quest

lines.speaker: Stanford

sections.title: Talking to Gossip after the quest
Talking to the Poison Salesman after the quest

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: I hear you're pretty smart to have solved the Sinclair Murder!

sections.title: Talking to the Poison Salesman after the quest if the player has progress in Fremennik Trials

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
